name: Veterans Crisis Line
description: 24/7 confidential crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families. Provides immediate intervention by trained VA responders available via phone, text, or online chat. Free and accessible to all veterans regardless of VA enrollment status.
notes: 24/7/365 availability. No VA enrollment required. Serves all eras of veterans.

name: VA Mental Health Services
description: Comprehensive mental health services available at all VA Medical Centers nationwide. Offers outpatient care, telehealth, residential rehabilitation, and evidence-based therapies including CPT, PE, and EMDR for PTSD treatment.
notes: Over 1.7 million veterans received mental health services at VA last year. No copays for first 3 outpatient mental health visits per calendar year through 2027.

name: Vet Centers (Readjustment Counseling Services)
description: Community-based counseling centers providing readjustment counseling, individual/family therapy, and peer support. Over 300 centers nationwide plus 83 Mobile Vet Centers. Services include marriage, family, group, and individual counseling at no cost.
notes: Available to all combat-zone veterans and their families. Established by Congress in 1979. Mobile Vet Centers serve rural areas.

name: Wounded Warrior Project - Mental Wellness
description: Comprehensive mental health program providing no-cost services including individual counseling, peer support groups, and specialized treatment programs. Programs range from telephonic support (WWP Talk) to intensive 2-week brain health programs at top medical centers.
notes: Serves wounded warriors through Warrior Care Network partnership with Emory, Mass General, Rush, and UCLA. Project Odyssey offers 12-week adventure-based program.

name: Cohen Veterans Network
description: National integrated network of mental health clinics providing client-centered therapy and comprehensive case management. Free services with insurance or sliding scale. 19 clinics nationwide serving post-9/11 veterans, active duty, and military families.
notes: Celebrating 10-year anniversary in 2026. Brief, evidence-based therapy focusing on post-9/11 veterans. No cost if uninsured.

name: Give an Hour
description: Provides confidential, no-cost mental health care to active-duty military, veterans, and their families. Founded in 2005, offers one-on-one counseling, peer support groups, and virtual peer support programs led by trained peers with lived experience.
notes: Founded by Dr. Barbara Van Dahlen. Virtual peer support groups available. Free, confidential services.

name: The Headstrong Project
description: Nonprofit providing cost-free, stigma-free, confidential mental health treatment for post-9/11 veterans and families. Offers 30 free sessions using evidence-based treatments (CPT, EMDR, PE). Hybrid in-person and telehealth model in 47 states.
notes: 16 core markets with national telehealth reach. 97% client return rate after first session. 89% report treatment effectiveness. Provided 44,000+ free sessions in 2024.

name: Home Base Program
description: National nonprofit providing world-class clinical care, wellness, education, and research at no cost. Intensive Clinical Program compresses 2 years of treatment into intensive 2-week program. Serves veterans from all 50 states and territories.
notes: Founded 2009 by Red Sox Foundation and MGH. Served 45,000+ veterans. Free services mostly philanthropically funded.

name: TAPS (Tragedy Assistance Program for Survivors)
description: National nonprofit providing compassionate care and resources to families grieving death in military/veteran community. 24/7 peer support network, regional seminars, youth programs, and retreats. Free services to all grieving military families regardless of circumstances.
notes: Operating since 1994. Welcomed 8,911 new survivors in 2024. Annual National Military Survivor Seminar held Memorial Day weekend.

name: Team Red, White & Blue
description: Community-based wellness nonprofit focusing on physical health, mental health, purpose, and relationships. Evidence-based programs demonstrating 25% improvement in physical health and 21% in mental health. Over 200,000 members nationwide.
notes: Founded 2010. Focuses on connection through physical wellness activities. Research-backed outcomes using biomarkers and wearable tech.

name: Travis Manion Foundation
description: Veteran wellness organization providing Spartan Leadership Program, 7-month proprietary program connecting passion with purpose. Participants show 21% increase in thriving scores. 24-hour access to online mental health resources and counseling through Spartan Development Center.
notes: Focuses on isolation and finding renewed purpose. Addresses 20+ veteran suicides daily. All support resources available 24/7.

name: Stop Soldier Suicide
description: Only national nonprofit exclusively focused on reducing military and veteran suicide. ROGER wellness service provides evidence-based, suicide-specific care including crisis intervention, safety planning, and virtual counseling. Founded by veterans in 2010.
notes: Exclusively focused on suicide prevention. Evidence-based, suicide-specific care. Free, confidential services.

name: The Mission Continues
description: Empowers veterans to continue service while strengthening communities. Peer support organization filling mental health service gaps through community engagement and social connection. 85% of participants report feeling more connected to veterans after program completion.
notes: Service-based approach to mental health. Combats isolation and depression through purpose-driven activities.

name: Blue Star Families
description: Supports military and veteran families through nonclinical peer-based mental health programming. Blue Star Support Circles deliver structured 8-week virtual cohorts funded by Parker Gordon Fox Suicide Prevention Grant. Combat the Silence campaign addresses mental health stigma.
notes: 275,000 members touching 1.5 million annually. 16% decrease in those feeling services inaccessible. Partnership with Red Cross, PsychArmor, Veterans Yoga Project.

name: Military OneSource
description: 24/7 gateway to mental health resources for active-duty service members and families. Provides up to 12 solution-focused counseling sessions per issue. Available in-person, by phone, video, or chat. Military Crisis Line accessible via 988 then press 1.
notes: Also includes BeThere peer support program. For transitioning veterans up to 365 days post-separation.

name: BeThere Peer Support Program
description: DoD program providing confidential peer-to-peer support via phone, text, and chat. Staffed by veteran service members and military spouses. Helps with everyday problem-solving and career/life challenges. Free and confidential 24/7.
notes: Staffed by veterans and military family members. Partners with Military OneSource.

name: VA Polytrauma/TBI System of Care
description: Integrated network of specialized rehabilitation programs for veterans with traumatic brain injury and polytrauma. Provides interdisciplinary evaluation, treatment, case management, patient/family education, and advanced rehabilitation.
notes: Specialized for TBI and complex polytrauma cases. Integrated with VHA mental health services.

name: Defense and Veterans Brain Injury Center (DVBIC)
description: Collaboration between DoD, VA, and Brain Injury Association ensuring active duty and veterans with brain injury receive evaluation, treatment, and follow-up. Founded 1992. Provides comprehensive TBI resources and treatment coordination.
notes: Tripartite collaboration: DoD, VA, and BIAA. Established 1992.

name: Shepherd Center SHARE Military Initiative
description: Evidence-based rehabilitation for military veterans, service members, and first responders with TBI and mental health concerns. Operating since 2007. Comprehensive inpatient and outpatient rehabilitation services.
notes: Specialized TBI rehabilitation center. Operating since 2007.

name: The Soldiers Project
description: Volunteer licensed mental health professionals providing free, unlimited, confidential therapy to post-9/11 veterans and their families. Founded 2010. Therapists include psychiatrists, psychologists, social workers, and marriage counselors.
notes: Volunteer-based, 100% donated services. Unlimited therapy sessions. Locations in CA, IL, PA, WA, WY, TX, NY.

name: NAMI (National Alliance on Mental Illness)
description: NAMI Homefront program designed for family, caregivers, and friends of veterans with mental health conditions. Free 6-session educational program. NAMI Connection Recovery Support Groups available. 24/7 online resources and peer support.
notes: NAMI Homefront specifically for military families. Free programs. National affiliate network.

name: U.S.VETS - Mental Health and Wellness Services
description: National leader in providing integrated housing, employment, and mental health services. Free counseling and treatment in safe, confidential environment. Offers physical wellness activities, mindfulness activities, life skills groups and classes.
notes: Integrated services combining housing, employment, and mental health. Comprehensive wellness approach.

name: Operation Enduring Warrior
description: Veteran-founded nonprofit 100% volunteer-run honoring and empowering wounded veterans through physical, mental, and emotional rehabilitation. Programs include Adventure, Archery, Athletic Events, and Skydiving providing rigorous challenges for healing.
notes: Adventure-based programs for wounded veterans. 100% volunteer organization.

name: Coaching Into Care
description: National VA telephone service helping family members encourage veterans to seek mental health treatment. Licensed psychologists and social workers provide up to 6 months of coaching. Free, confidential counseling for concerned family members and friends.
notes: Helps family members navigate how to talk to veterans about mental health. Associated with increased veteran mental health care access.

name: VA Residential Rehabilitation Treatment Programs
description: Comprehensive 24/7 residential treatment for veterans with severe mental health conditions. About 250 programs at 120 sites nationwide accommodating 6,500+ veterans. Typically 6-week stays with 4+ hours daily treatment. Available in all 50 states including Alaska and Hawaii.
notes: Intensive inpatient programs. Most veterans must be enrolled in VA health care. Stays can range 2 weeks to several months.

name: VA PTSD National Center for PTSD
description: Leading federal center for PTSD research, education, and training. Provides evidence-based treatment information, peer support resources, professional training, and access to PTSD specialists at VA facilities nationwide.
notes: Federal research and education center. Comprehensive PTSD treatment information.

name: VA Telehealth Services
description: VA Video Connect app and phone-based telehealth services providing secure videoconferencing access to VA providers. Virtual group therapy sessions and individual sessions. No travel required.
notes: Removes travel barriers. Smartphone, tablet, or computer with internet connection required.

name: Military Sexual Trauma (MST) Services - VA
description: Comprehensive VA-wide MST services including outpatient care at every medical center, residential/inpatient treatment, peer support, and MST coordinators at all facilities. Free 'Beyond MST' app for self-help. Confidential services available regardless of discharge status.
notes: Estimated 1 in 3 female and 1 in 50 male veterans experience MST. Separate programs for men and women where available.

name: Women Veterans Mental Health Services - VA
description: Women-centered mental health services at all VA medical centers. Every facility has Women Veteran Program Manager and Women's Mental Health Champion. Trauma-focused therapies including CPT, PE, EMDR. Specialized residential programs for women veterans.
notes: Gender-sensitive treatment. Some programs women-only. Specialized for women's mental health needs.

name: Soldiers for Life Transition Assistance Program (SFL-TAP)
description: Army's transition program for separating soldiers and families. Provides benefits information, budgeting, job search skills, career guidance. Mandatory for soldiers with 180+ continuous duty days. Includes 24/7 call centers with trained personnel.
notes: Mandatory transition program. Should begin 365 days before separation. Retirees begin 2 years before.

name: Volunteer Services - VA
description: VA peer support programs where certified peer specialists trained with lived recovery experience provide hope and modeling. Over 1,350 peer specialists across VHA programs nationwide.
notes: Peer specialists working in intensive community mental health, substance use treatment, homeless programs, vocational rehab.

name: SAMHSA National Helpline
description: Free, confidential, 24/7/365 treatment referral and information service for substance use and mental health disorders. Connects callers to state services and local treatment resources. Available in English and Spanish.
notes: Available to veterans and general population. Free referral service. 24/7 availability.

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline
description: National 24/7 suicide and crisis lifeline providing free, confidential support. Press 1 for veterans (Veterans Crisis Line) or call without extension for general population. Available via phone, text, and online chat.
notes: Serves both veterans and general population. Redesigned hotline for easier access.

name: VA Compensated Work Therapy (CWT)
description: VA clinical vocational rehabilitation program at every medical center supporting veterans with mental illness/physical disabilities. Three components: Supported Employment, Community Based Employment Services, and Transitional Work.
notes: Vocational rehabilitation supporting employment for veterans with mental health barriers.

name: Equine-Assisted Therapy Programs
description: Equine-assisted activities and therapies for veterans with PTSD and mental health conditions. Programs include mindfulness-based horse therapy, EAGALA-model psychotherapy, and trauma-informed coaching. Research shows benefits for symptom reduction.
notes: Evidence-based alternative therapy. VA Salt Lake City operates Horses Helping Veterans program.

name: Lifeline Equine Therapy Services (LETS)
description: Incorporates rescued horses into healing programs for veterans. EAGALA-model psychotherapy, certified professional life coaching, and trauma-informed yoga. Serves veterans, military personnel, VA caregivers, and first responders.
notes: Combines rescue horses with therapy. EAGALA-certified model. (Original provider website offline — use EAGALA directory to find certified equine therapy practitioners: eagala.org/find-a-professional)

name: Veterans Yoga Project
description: Trauma-informed yoga and mindfulness programs specifically designed for veterans. Classes and online programs. Research-backed approach reducing PTSD and depression symptoms.
notes: Trauma-informed approach. Partner with Blue Star Families.

name: The American Legion - Veterans Services
description: Service organization providing free assistance with VA disability benefits, counseling referrals, education, and employment resources. Service officers guide veterans through VA process. Works with Vet Centers for mental health services.
notes: Founded 1919. Connects veterans with no-cost VA services. Network of service officers nationwide.

name: DAV (Disabled American Veterans)
description: Organization of disabled veterans providing free assistance with VA claims and benefits, transportation to VA appointments, and mental health advocacy. Volunteer corps available for support.
notes: 1.3 million members. Free transportation to VA appointments.

name: VFW (Veterans of Foreign Wars)
description: Military service organization providing veterans with grants, scholarships, community support, and advocacy. Nationwide network of posts offering peer support and fellowship.
notes: Founded 1899. Nationwide post network.

name: Volunteers of America
description: Nonprofit providing food, housing, and counseling to veterans. Services address acute and long-term issues including crisis intervention, budgeting, employment, and recreation. 31 affiliates nationwide.
notes: Founded 1896. Serves 1.5 million people annually. Large veteran residential programs in major cities.

name: National Veterans Legal Services Program
description: Provides free legal services and VA benefits counseling to low-income veterans. Helps with benefits appeals, housing, and other legal issues affecting mental health outcomes.
notes: Free legal services. Serves low-income veterans.

name: Noncommissioned Officers Association (NCOA)
description: Military Service Organization founded 1960 for enlisted service members and veterans. Focuses on quality of life, mental health, physical health, education, and workforce development advocacy.
notes: Founded 1960. Advocacy organization on Capitol Hill. Disaster relief and scholarship programs.

name: Patriot Support Programs
description: 30+ dedicated programs throughout U.S. for military and veterans. Access to hundreds of facilities with specialized services for mental health and substance abuse treatment.
notes: Network of military-focused treatment facilities.

name: Operation Iraq and Afghanistan Veterans (OIF/OEF)
description: Peer support and resources specifically for veterans of Iraq and Afghanistan operations. Focuses on combat-related mental health issues and readjustment support.
notes: Specifically for OIF/OEF era veterans. Combat zone operations focus.

name: Veterans Health Administration (VHA) Primary Care
description: VHA primary care services integrated with mental health. Behavioral Health Integration (BHIP) model coordinates care across providers. Available at all VA medical centers.
notes: Integrated care model. Behavioral Health Integration Team at most centers.

name: LifeStance Health - Veterans Mental Health Services
description: Private telehealth and in-person mental health services specializing in military and veterans services. Operates in 33 states including California, New York, and Texas.
notes: Private telehealth provider. Insurance and self-pay options.

name: Henry M. Jackson Foundation - Military Medicine Research
description: Conducts military medical research including mental health outcomes studies. Provides evidence-based information for veteran mental health treatment and policy.
notes: DoD research partner. Evidence-based treatment research.

name: Syracuse University - Institute for Veterans and Military Families (IVMF)
description: Leading university institute researching veteran wellness and transition. Provides evidence-based resources and policy recommendations for veteran mental health.
notes: Academic research institution. Evidence-based veteran wellness research.

name: National Center for PTSD - Clinical Practice Guidelines
description: VA/DoD 2023 Clinical Practice Guidelines for PTSD management. Evidence-based recommendations for Prolonged Exposure (PE), Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), and EMDR treatments.
notes: Updated 2023. Evidence-based treatment recommendations.

name: Moral Injury Outreach and Support Program
description: VA program addressing moral injury - psychological distress from witnessing or perpetrating actions violating personal values. Available through VA mental health services nationwide.
notes: Specialized for moral injury treatment. Available at most VA centers.

name: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA)
description: Federal agency providing substance abuse and mental health treatment resources, referrals, and support. National Directory of mental health and substance abuse treatment facilities.
notes: Federal resource. Treatment facility locator available.

name: Veterans Benefits Administration
description: Provides disability compensation, pension, and survivor benefits for service-connected conditions including mental health. Coordinates with VHA for care.
notes: Benefits system for service-connected disabilities.

name: Center for Women Veterans
description: VA center ensuring women veterans receive comprehensive care. Every facility has Women Veteran Program Manager. Advocates for women's mental health services and gender-sensitive care.
notes: Gender-sensitive advocacy. Program Managers at all VA centers.

name: Office of Mental Health and Suicide Prevention - VA
description: VA's top clinical priority office overseeing national suicide prevention strategy. Implements Governor's and Mayor's Challenges. Coordinates Staff Sergeant Parker Gordon Fox Suicide Prevention Grant Program.
notes: National suicide prevention coordination. Grants for community programs.

name: MyHealtheVet - VA Patient Portal
description: VA's online patient portal allowing secure communication with VA providers, appointment scheduling, accessing medical records, and prescription management. Facilitates telehealth coordination.
notes: Digital patient portal. Secure messaging with providers.

name: VA Community-Based Outpatient Clinics (CBOCs)
description: VA primary care and mental health clinics located closer to veterans' homes. Over 600 CBOCs nationwide providing outpatient mental health services including psychiatry and counseling.
notes: Expanded access to mental health care outside major medical centers.

name: VA Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
description: For VA employees offering confidential counseling, referral services, and support. While designed for VA staff, many benefits support veteran service providers' mental health.
notes: VA staff mental health support. Ensures quality providers for veterans.

name: Grief Support for Veteran Families
description: Multiple organizations providing grief support for families of fallen service members and deceased veterans. TAPS is primary national resource; local VA Vet Centers also provide grief counseling.
notes: TAPS leading provider. VA Vet Centers provide grief counseling.

name: Going for Gold - Combat Veteran PTSD Programs
description: Adventure-based retreat programs for combat veterans with PTSD. Mountain retreats and expeditions promoting healing through outdoor activities and peer support.
notes: Adventure-based approach. Limited verified online presence.

name: VA Caregiver Support Program
description: Supports family members and caregivers of veterans with service-connected disabilities. Provides stipends, training, counseling, and mental health services for caregivers managing veteran mental health conditions.
notes: Federal caregiver support program. Stipends available for qualified caregivers.

name: Military and Family Life Counseling (MFLC)
description: Free, confidential non-medical counseling for active duty, Reserve, Guard members and families. Available at military installations. Addresses relationship, parenting, financial, and work issues.
notes: Military installation-based service. Transitioning veterans may have access.

name: Healthy Coping Resources App
description: VA mobile applications providing self-help tools, crisis resources, and coping strategies. Apps like Beyond MST, PTSD Coach, and others available free on app stores.
notes: Multiple free apps available. Digital mental health tools.

name: Make the Connection - VA Mental Health Resource
description: VA's online resource featuring veteran mental health stories, information about conditions, treatment options, and local resources. Interactive tool helping veterans understand mental health challenges.
notes: Veteran storytelling platform. Condition-specific resources.

name: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health (NCCIH)
description: Federal resource providing information on complementary therapies including yoga, mindfulness, and acupuncture for PTSD and mental health. Evidence-based complementary approach.
notes: Federal complementary health information. Evidence-based.

name: Veterans Peer Network Groups - VA
description: VA-sponsored peer support groups where veterans with similar experiences meet for mutual support. Available online and in-person at VA facilities. Drop-in groups available.
notes: Peer-led support groups. Available at most VA centers.

name: Go ArmyEd - Army Benefits Information
description: Army educational benefits information supporting career transitions and continuing education for veterans, reducing transition-related stress and anxiety.
notes: Army-specific benefits education. Career pathway support.

name: Military OneSource Parenting Support
description: Military OneSource provides parenting support resources and counseling addressing family dynamics, children's mental health, and supporting military parents through stress.
notes: Family-focused support. Up to 12 sessions per issue.

name: Veterans Legal Services
description: Pro bono legal services helping low-income veterans with benefits appeals, disability claims, and housing issues. Addresses legal stress contributing to mental health challenges.
notes: Free legal services. Low-income veteran focus.

name: Family Resource Centers - Military Communities
description: Community resource centers at military installations providing family support, counseling referrals, and mental health resources for military families.
notes: Installation-based family support. Military-connected services.

name: Red Cross Services for Military Families
description: American Red Cross providing emergency assistance, financial support, and counseling referrals for military families. Disaster assistance and emergency communication.
notes: Emergency family support. Disaster assistance.

name: USO Services for Veterans
description: USO providing services to military members and veterans including emergency financial assistance, programs, and community support.
notes: Military service organization. Emergency assistance programs.

name: Resilience Training Programs
description: VA and DoD resilience training programs teaching coping skills, stress management, and mental health awareness to service members and veterans.
notes: Prevention-focused training. Military-integrated approach.

name: Combat Stress Conference
description: Annual conference on combat stress and PTSD research bringing together professionals and veterans. Provides training and cutting-edge information on PTSD treatment.
notes: Professional conference. Limited verified current information.

name: Integrative Health and Wellness Programs - VA
description: VA integrative health programs combining conventional and complementary approaches including acupuncture, meditation, yoga, and massage therapy for mental health conditions.
notes: Integrated care approach. Available at select VA centers.

name: Mental Health America (MHA)
description: National nonprofit providing mental health screening tools, educational resources, and veteran-specific information on mental health conditions and treatment options.
notes: Free mental health screening. Veteran-focused resources.

name: National Suicide Prevention Lifeline
description: National crisis support available to all Americans. Press 1 for Veterans Crisis Line. 24/7 phone, chat, and text support by trained counselors.
notes: Serves general population and veterans. 988 redesign for accessibility.

name: Faith-Based Veteran Support Programs
description: Various faith-based organizations providing spiritual support, counseling, and community for veterans. Partnerships with VA Vet Centers for faith-integrated care.
notes: Faith-based mental health support. Integration with VA services varies.

name: Workplace Veteran Mental Health Programs
description: Corporate EAP and HR programs supporting veteran employees with mental health services, accommodations, and peer support. Many large employers have veteran employee resource groups.
notes: Employer-based support. Available through company benefits.

name: Online Therapy Platforms - Veteran Specialists
description: Private online therapy platforms (BetterHelp, Talkspace, etc.) offering licensed therapists with military experience. Insurance or self-pay options. Accessible from home.
notes: Private pay options. Various credential levels. Research provider credentials.

name: State Veterans Offices Mental Health Coordinators
description: All 50 states have Veterans Offices with mental health coordinators connecting veterans to state and federal resources. Free benefits counseling and service coordination.
notes: State-level services. Free benefits coordination.

name: County and Local Veterans Services
description: County and local governments provide veterans services including benefits counseling, referrals, and mental health support coordination. Often have service officers.
notes: Local-level services. County service officers available.

name: University Veteran Support Programs
description: Many U.S. colleges and universities have dedicated veteran student services including counseling, support groups, and mental health resources for veteran students.
notes: Campus-based services. Available to veteran students.

name: Hospital-Based Trauma Centers
description: Major medical centers and trauma hospitals providing specialized care for veterans with TBI and trauma-related conditions. Many partner with VA for integrated services.
notes: Private medical centers. Insurance/self-pay. VA partnerships common.

name: Community Mental Health Centers
description: Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) and community mental health centers providing sliding-scale mental health services to veterans and underserved populations.
notes: Sliding scale services. Community-based access. 211 referral line.

name: Mission Within
description: 6-week clinical psychedelic program pairing online therapy, coaching, preparation, and integration with a 4-day in-person medicine retreat. Founded by Dr. Martín Polanco, serving 1,300+ special operations veterans and spouses with ~80% no longer meeting PTSD diagnostic criteria.
notes: Legal status depends on retreat location; uses 5-MeO-DMT or psilocybin where permitted

name: Heroic Hearts Project
description: 501(c)(3) veteran organization providing carefully guided psilocybin retreat programs created by veterans for veterans. Hosts retreats in Oregon (legal jurisdiction) and anticipates Colorado 2026. Helped 1,500+ veterans significantly reduce PTSD symptoms.
notes: Legal psilocybin retreats in Oregon where therapy is licensed; PTSD has killed more soldiers by suicide than combat since 9/11

name: Johns Hopkins Center for Psychedelic and Consciousness Research
description: Leading research center opened in 2019 with 40+ researchers studying psilocybin effects. First federal grant for psychedelic research in 50 years. Recruiting for smoking cessation (80 participants) and other conditions.
notes: Clinical trials for PTSD being conducted; out-of-state applicants preferred within reasonable driving distance

name: MAPS (Multidisciplinary Association for Psychedelic Studies) MDMA-Assisted Therapy
description: Phase 3 clinical trials completed showing 88% of severe PTSD participants experienced clinically significant reduction. FDA designated as Breakthrough Therapy. 6-8 hour medication sessions followed by 90-minute integration sessions (3 cycles = 18 weeks total).
notes: NOT yet FDA-approved for clinical use; requires 100+ hours training for therapists; requires 2 providers minimum per session

name: Behold Retreats - Psilocybin Costa Rica
description: Eco-friendly psilocybin retreat in Monteverde, Costa Rica offering 6-7 day programs with small vetted groups. Expert guides and healers with decades of expertise. Solar-powered healing sanctuary with ocean/jungle views.
notes: Legal psilocybin in Costa Rica; screening for psychotic disorders conducted; personalized 1:1 guidance included

name: Cambridge Biotherapies
description: Leading provider of ketamine infusion therapy and psychedelic-assisted psychotherapy in Massachusetts. Trauma-informed approach with clinicians trained in trauma impact. IV ketamine can reduce PTSD symptoms within hours or days.
contact_info: VA Community Care eligible; contact directly or through VA referral
notes: VA coverage available through Community Care; FDA-approved ketamine off-label for PTSD; also offers TMS

name: McLean Hospital Ketamine Service
description: Part of Harvard Medical School, provides IV ketamine infusion therapy in a clinical setting. Evidence-based treatment for rapid symptom reduction in treatment-resistant cases.
notes: Off-label ketamine for PTSD; requires psychiatric evaluation; insurance coverage varies

name: Spravato (Esketamine Nasal Spray)
description: FDA-approved 2019 for treatment-resistant depression. Administered in certified medical facility under observation; 2-hour monitoring post-administration required. VA provides coverage case-by-case for eligible veterans.

location.state: VA medical centers (selected) and private clinics

contact_info: Contact local VA mental health or VA Community Care
notes: FDA-approved for MDD/suicidal ideation; VA began rollout 2019; requires 2+ failed antidepressants; flexible protocols as of Feb 2022

name: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy (HBOT) - Healing with Hyperbarics
description: Non-invasive treatment in pressurized chamber breathing 100% pure oxygen. Research shows 40 sessions improve memory, attention, anxiety, depression, and reduce suicidal thoughts. One free treatment day monthly for veterans.
notes: VA approved in AL, FL, GA, MD, NJ, TX, OK only; FDA not approved for mental health; insurance rarely covers

name: Oxygen Oasis - Veteran HBOT Program
description: Nominate a veteran to receive free hyperbaric oxygen therapy. Focuses on providing HBOT for service-connected injuries and PTSD symptoms.
notes: Free HBOT for nominated veterans; apply via website

name: Stellate Ganglion Block (SGB) - WellStone
description: Outpatient nerve block procedure injecting local anesthetic to stellate ganglion. Evidence-level 1 from multi-center RCT showing twice the effect of placebo. 30-minute procedure with immediate results possible.
notes: WellStone provides free SGB to veterans/first responders since 2021; Brian McLean pioneered veteran SGB use at Tripler Army Medical Center

name: Stellate Ganglion Block - VA Providers
description: SGB procedure performed by VA-trained providers at VA medical centers. Pioneered by Dr. Colin Fernandes (VA Northern California) and teams at Walter Reed, Ft. Bragg, and other military medical centers.
contact_info: Contact local VA neurology or anesthesia department
notes: JAMA Psychiatry published gold-standard Level 1 evidence (2019); double the effect vs. placebo

name: Homecoming for Veterans - Neurofeedback
description: Partnership between EEG Institute and Homecoming offering free neurofeedback (EEG biofeedback) to veterans nationally through a network of clinicians. Over 60% of participants no longer meet PTSD criteria after treatment.
notes: Free for veterans; trains brain to self-regulate brainwaves; 3-month intensive intervention showed sustained benefits

name: RenuYou Neurofeedback
description: Neurofeedback center offering EEG-based brain training for veterans. Tri-Care insurance and Canadian veterans' health programs accepted.
notes: Accepts Tri-Care; specializes in veteran populations

name: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS) - VA Programs
description: Non-invasive magnetic stimulation of brain neurons in prefrontal cortex. VA data: 41.4% response rate for MDD, 65.3% PTSD symptom reduction, 46.1% no longer meeting PTSD threshold after adequate treatment.
notes: No surgery/sedation required; deep TMS for treatment-resistant depression; established protocol with published outcomes

name: TMS - Newport TMS
description: Private TMS provider specializing in veterans with PTSD and depression. Outpatient procedure with no sedation or surgery.
notes: Some clinics accept VA referrals and insurance; Medicare coverage available

name: EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) - VA
description: Evidence-based psychotherapy using bilateral eye movements, sounds, or tapping while processing trauma. VA/DOD lists as 'best practice.' Typically 50-90 minute sessions, weekly for ~3 months. Low-cost/free through VA.

location.state: VA Vet Centers and VA medical centers nationwide

notes: Strongest recommendation in clinical practice guidelines; doesn't require extensive trauma discussion; telehealth available

name: Amen Clinics - SPECT Neuroimaging
description: Uses SPECT brain imaging (94% accuracy distinguishing PTSD from TBI vs. MRI/CT 'normal' findings). Named Discover Magazine's Top 100 science discoveries 2015. Combines neuroimaging with comprehensive psychiatric treatment.
notes: 30 years SPECT research; 80 peer-reviewed studies published; HBOT also offered

name: Returning Home - Somatic Experiencing Retreat
description: 6-day free Somatic Experiencing trauma healing retreat for 30 veterans with PTSD. Integrates SE with equine therapy and wellness practices. Includes lodging, meals, travel stipend. Focuses on nervous system re-regulation.
notes: Free retreat; SE doesn't require full trauma retelling; nervous system regulation focus; equine component included

name: Freespira - Breathing-Based PTSD Treatment
description: Breathing-based treatment program correcting irregular breathing patterns underlying PTSD and anxiety. Portable device allows home-based treatment with professional oversight.
notes: FDA-cleared device; corrects physiologic factors in PTSD; home-based with provider support

name: Warriors at Ease
description: Nonprofit founded 2011 with 800+ trauma-sensitive yoga and meditation teachers. Serves 16,000+ service members, veterans, families annually. T3 fellowship program trains veterans as yoga teachers.

location.city: 800+ locations in USA and abroad (VA facilities

notes: Evidence-based practices; available in VA facilities, Vet Centers, military bases; offers teacher training fellowship

name: Connected Warriors
description: Trauma-conscious yoga nonprofit offering free classes to service members, veterans, and families. 24 states. Classes include breathwork, meditation, mindful movement, gratitude, rest. Defense Dept study showed PTSD symptom reduction after 10 weeks.
notes: Free services; VA and DoD funded research; military-specific trauma training

name: Veterans Yoga Project - Mindful Resilience for Trauma Recovery
description: Evidence-informed yoga program with five-part structure: breathwork, meditation, mindful movement, gratitude, rest. Integrated in mental health and addiction treatment programs. Classes based on combat veteran research.
notes: Clinically tested; integrated in residential addiction treatment; MRT protocol evidence-informed

name: Warrior Surf Foundation
description: 501(c)(3) providing free surf therapy, wellness coaching, and yoga to veterans struggling with mental health. Learning to surf builds self-regulation skills transferable to PTSD/depression management.
notes: Free services; 58% PTSD/MDD remission after 6 weeks; 59% general anxiety reduction post-surf event

name: Veteran Surf Project (Australia)
description: Registered charity delivering free 10-week learn-to-surf courses followed by continuing weekly sessions. Research shows statistically significant post-program reductions in PTSD, depression, anxiety at 7-month follow-up.
notes: Australian program; research-backed; combines ocean, physical activity, social bonding

name: Equine Therapy - VA Programs
description: Small group equine-assisted therapy with mental health professional and equine specialist. Veterans groom/lead horses in trust-building exercises. Over 6,200 veterans served through 800 certified centers. 50%+ showed marked PTSD/depression reduction.
notes: Horses provide nonjudgmental feedback; 66,000+ children/adults served; evidence-based approach

name: Huts for Vets
description: Wilderness therapy retreats for veterans and active-duty combining guided hikes, group discussions, community building. Nature-based approach reduces stress, anxiety, PTSD through outdoor immersion.
notes: Wilderness therapy; outdoor immersion reduces PTSD symptoms and stress

name: Outward Bound for Veterans
description: Challenging wilderness expeditions (backpacking, rock climbing, whitewater rafting) with group therapy and personal reflection. Builds camaraderie, confidence, and clarity. Many courses cover travel for combat-deployed veterans.

location.state: Multiple US locations (regional Outward Bound schools)

notes: Tuition covered; many courses cover travel; strengthens peer relationships; long-term benefits

name: Soltara Healing Center - Ayahuasca Costa Rica
description: Works exclusively with ayahuasca plant medicine and Indigenous Peruvian Shipibo healers. Permitted by Costa Rican Ministry of Health for alternative/natural therapies. Focuses on integrating traditional healing with modern understanding.
notes: Legal in Costa Rica; Shipibo healers; Ministry of Health permit; retreat-based format

name: New Life Rising - Ayahuasca Costa Rica
description: Longest-running ayahuasca retreat in Costa Rica (since 2014). Honors traditional practices while prioritizing safety. Small group retreats with experienced facilitators.
notes: 14+ years operation; small groups; traditional + safety focus; legal in Costa Rica

name: Rythmia - Medically Licensed Ayahuasca
description: Only medically licensed facility in world authorized to serve ayahuasca therapeutically. Licensed by Costa Rican Ministry of Health. 2 doctors, 6 nurses, on-site clinic, medi-vac helicopter. 17,000+ guests served. Blends plant medicine with yoga, breathwork, meditation.
notes: ONLY medically licensed ayahuasca facility; Ministry of Health licensed; medical staff on-site; luxury facilities; some critical reviews exist

name: Arkana Ayahuasca Retreats - Peru
description: 10+ years experience guiding thousands through ayahuasca ceremonies in Peru. Two retreat centers: Amazon rainforest and Sacred Valley. Authentic Shipibo healing tradition. 1,000+ five-star reviews. Perfect safety record reported.
notes: Long-term operation; traditional Shipibo healers; excellent reviews; Amazon and Andean locations

name: Beond Ibogaine - Cancun, Mexico
description: Neuroplasticity treatment center blending medical facility standards with mental health treatment and psychedelic retreat services. Treats fentanyl, opioid, stimulant addictions, plus PTSD and anxiety.
notes: Ibogaine legal in Mexico; medical facility standards; addiction + mental health focus; highly popular with veterans

name: MindScape Retreat - Ibogaine Cozumel
description: Ibogaine-assisted therapy for chemical dependency, depression, PTSD, anxiety. All-inclusive programs starting at $6,500. Specializes in resetting neural pathways for addiction and trauma.
notes: All-inclusive retreat; ibogaine + mental health integration; popular for opioid addiction + PTSD

name: Transcend Ibogaine Clinic - Mexico
description: Recognized as one of best ibogaine treatment centers for North American clients. Treats opioid, alcohol, prescription drug addictions plus anxiety, depression, PTSD through neural pathway restructuring.
notes: Highly regarded for North American veterans; ibogaine resets opiate receptors; integration support included

name: BraveMind - Virtual Reality Therapy
description: VA-partnered VR exposure therapy creating 14 immersive 'worlds' for Iraq/Afghanistan veterans. 29 VA medical centers received donation. 1450+ VR headsets deployed across 165+ VA medical centers. 66-90% success rate. 2-week treatment shows 12-week exposure therapy effects.
notes: VA-deployed; recreates trauma scenes; customized to individual incidents; rapid symptom reduction

name: Emory Healthcare Veterans Program - VR Therapy
description: Free VR exposure therapy for post-9/11 veterans and service members nationwide, regardless of discharge status. Uses VR to recreate trauma scenes in therapeutic context with trained clinicians.
notes: Free and confidential; nationwide access; pioneered VR + exposure therapy for PTSD

name: Floatation Therapy (Sensory Deprivation)
description: REST (Restricted Environmental Stimulation) in sensory deprivation tanks with Epsom salt solution. Research shows large post-float reductions in PTSD fatigue, anxiety, depression. Calms fight-or-flight response.
notes: Research shows 22% cortisol drop after 8 sessions; drug-free alternative; not substitute for professional care

name: Wounded Warrior Project (WWP) - Multiple Programs
description: Comprehensive veteran mental health organization offering Project Odyssey (12-week adventure-based program), Warrior Care Network (2-week accelerated brain health at top medical centers), WWP Talk (listening support since 2013). Covers 100% of treatment, travel, meals.
notes: Multi-program approach; accelerated cohort treatment; listening support line; all costs covered

name: Boulder Crest Retreat - Warrior PATHH
description: 90-day non-pharmacological program beginning with 5-day intensive. Peer-delivered by trained combat veterans and civilians. Sustained improvements at 18 months: 50% depression, 45% anxiety, 40% stress reduction. PTSD scores reduced 56%.
notes: Free all-inclusive; 18-month sustained benefits documented; non-pharmaceutical approach; family programs available

name: Travis Mills Foundation
description: 7 unique free programs for post-9/11 injured veterans and families. Retreat center with 9,800 sq ft healing facility (pools, massage, fitness). Activities: kayaking, archery, dog sledding, swimming with animals. Virtual programs and ambassador program included.
notes: Entirely free; family-focused; unique experiential activities; Georgia Aquarium partnership

name: Outward Bound - Philadelphia & Chesapeake Bay Schools
description: Regional Outward Bound schools offering adventure therapy courses. Tuition covered; transportation often covered for combat veterans. Builds resilience through wilderness expeditions.
notes: Regional programs; full tuition coverage; strong veteran focus at multiple schools

name: Semper Fi Odyssey - Adventure Therapy
description: Week-long retreat for warriors and families emphasizing small-team goal development, realistic planning, resilience-building. Partnered with Outdoor Odyssey. Addresses obstacles and leverages unique military experiences.
notes: Related to Semper Fi & America's Fund; also offers mentorship and neurofeedback programs

name: Operation Mend - UCLA Intensive Program
description: 4-week intensive treatment program for service members/veterans with service-related mTBI and PTSD. Two weeks UCLA campus + two weeks telehealth peer support. Multidisciplinary team evaluation included. All costs covered including travel.
notes: UCLA/Warrior Care Network partner; all-inclusive; cohort model; free evaluation; family can attend

name: Solara Mental Health - San Diego
description: Trauma-focused, veteran-only psychiatric treatment center. Residential, PHP, and virtual IOP programs. Up to 10 individual therapy sessions/week with EMDR, TMS, DBT, CBT, biofeedback. VA Community Care covered.
notes: Veteran-only facility; VA CCN provider; all costs covered via VA; travel/housing included

name: Craig Hospital - Operation TBI Freedom
description: Long-term non-clinical case management for Colorado post-9/11 veterans with TBI. Military Support Specialists (Certified Brain Injury Specialists with military background) provide crisis management, mental health referrals, employment/education assistance, emergency aid.
notes: Non-clinical case management; 24-month typical support; weekly contact; military-experienced staff

name: Mindfulness Coach - VA App
description: Free VA-developed mobile app teaching mindfulness practices for PTSD, anxiety, depression. Available on iOS and Android. Includes guided exercises, tracking tools, psychoeducation.
notes: Created by VA National Center for PTSD; free download; evidence-based mindfulness content

name: Team Red, White & Blue - Meditation Programs
description: Three trademarked meditation techniques by Eagle Ambassador Tywan (Marine Corps vet): Water Flow Breathwork (emotional fluidity), Warrior's Grounding Meditation (spiritual clarity), Sacred Silence Journey (fear reduction). Designed for veteran-specific challenges.
notes: Veteran-designed techniques; reduces amygdala activity; community-based fitness/wellness focus

name: Veterans Exploring Treatment Solutions (VETS)
description: Enables veterans to access safe, clinically-guided psychedelic therapy in countries where treatments are legal or unregulated. Screening, preparation, integration, and ongoing support provided.
notes: Legal/safe access internationally; clinician-guided; preparation and integration included

name: VA National Center for PTSD
description: Federal resource providing evidence-based treatment information, professional training, and public resources. PTSD Coach Online offers free breathing, meditation, psychoeducation tools. Comprehensive treatment guidelines.
notes: Government gold-standard resource; free tools and apps; evidence-based treatment info; professional training available

name: Patriot PowerUP - TMS for Veterans
description: Private TMS provider specializing in veteran populations with PTSD and depression. Outpatient noninvasive magnetic stimulation therapy.
notes: Veteran-focused TMS provider; insurance/VA referral friendly

name: Pacific Mind Health - TMS for Veterans
description: TMS therapy specializing in veteran populations with combat-related PTSD and depression.
notes: Veteran-specialized TMS provider; insurance coverage options

name: Operation PTSD A Veteran's Group Inc.
description: Nonprofit of combat and non-combat veterans volunteering to offer peer-to-peer counseling, support groups, veteran outreach. Completely free. Focuses on community support model.
notes: Peer-led organization; combat veteran-to-veteran support; completely free; community-based

name: Solara Mental Health - Breathwork Program
description: Breathwork and holistic methods integrated with evidence-based therapies (EMDR, TMS, DBT, CBT). Trauma-informed approach for veterans.
notes: Integrated holistic/evidence-based; VA contracted; transportation/housing covered

name: Avesta Ketamine & Wellness
description: IV ketamine and Spravato (esketamine) therapy for veterans with PTSD and depression. Specializes in VA coverage navigation and Community Care referrals.
notes: VA Community Care eligible; Spravato and IV ketamine; insurance/VA coverage expertise

name: BellaVida TMS - Combat Depression & PTSD
description: TMS therapy specifically marketed to military and veterans for combat-related depression and PTSD. Fast relief from symptoms.
notes: Military-focused TMS provider; rapid symptom relief focus; insurance friendly

name: The Mission Within
description: Clinical program founded by Dr. Martín Polanco specializing in treatment for special operations veterans with TBI, PTSD, depression, and addiction. Since 2017, has treated 1,000+ special ops veterans with 80% no longer meeting PTSD diagnostic criteria.
notes: Ibogaine is Schedule I in US but legal in Mexico. Operates in legal gray area. Stanford-backed research confirms 88% PTSD symptom reduction.

name: Ambio Life Sciences
description: Next-generation medical clinic delivering specialized care for TBI, trauma, and complex conditions. Founded in 2021, operates with medical oversight. Stanford research involved 30 veterans showing 88% PTSD reduction, 87% depression reduction, 81% anxiety reduction.
notes: Uses magnesium-ibogaine combination to prevent cardiac complications. Published in Nature Mental Health (2025).

name: Beond Ibogaine Treatment
description: Advanced neuroplasticity treatment center in Cancun serving 3,000+ patients. Offers tiered pricing with zero-cost Beond Service program for qualifying veterans. Nine-week structured program for PTSD/TBI with Stanford-backed methodology.
contact_info: Available on website; expanding to Austin for aftercare
notes: Expanding to Austin. Offers special zero-cost veteran program. Expanding medical capacity.

name: Casa Santa Isabel
description: Only licensed, medically supervised ibogaine treatment center in Baja California. Over 30 years experience. Licensed pharmacy and lab on-site. 24/7 medical monitoring in Mediterranean-style villa. 80% one-year abstinence rate.

location.city: Rosarito Beach (20 min from San Diego)

notes: Veterans/military/first responders receive special discounts. Only certified clinic in Mexico with on-site pharmacy and lab.

name: Transcend Clinic
description: Specialized ibogaine treatment center serving US and international clients. Located in upscale area away from hotel zone. 7-10 day programs with medical supervision and comprehensive integration support.
notes: Longer programs available for opioid-dependent individuals. Comprehensive intake process.

name: Experience Ibogaine Treatment Center
description: Comprehensive ibogaine detox programs with 24/7 medical monitoring and structured aftercare. Locations in Tijuana and Rosarito Beach. Treats complex addiction and mental health issues including PTSD.
notes: Veterans discounts available. Multiple location options.

name: IbogaQuest
description: Leading ibogaine treatment center founded in 2009 pioneering ibogaine therapy for addiction. Located in spiritual setting in Tepoztlán. Comprehensive medical supervision and integration support.
notes: 16+ years of experience. Scenic mountain location supports integration work.

name: Sayulita Wellness Retreat
description: Ranked #1 ibogaine treatment clinic in Mexico. Located 45 min from Puerto Vallarta. Offers 5-day focused reset and 7-day extended detox with integration. All-inclusive with comprehensive medical and therapeutic care.
notes: Comprehensive program includes ibogaine and psilocybin options. Coastal setting supports healing.

name: New Path Ibogaine Clinic
description: Safe and effective ibogaine treatment focusing on professional care and lasting results. Discounts available for therapeutic patients, first responders, and cash payments.
notes: First responder/veteran discounts available. Specialized in lasting outcomes.

name: Lifes Oasis Vallarta
description: Expert addiction recovery and mental health center located by ocean. Personalized care with holistic therapies and coastal surroundings supporting healing.
notes: Ocean-front location enhances therapeutic setting.

name: Arkana International Spiritual Center - Mexico (Izamal, Yucatan)
treatment_type: Ayahuasca ceremonies with psilocybin and temazcal
description: Seven-day retreat including three ayahuasca ceremonies, one psilocybin mushroom ceremony, and temazcal session. Partnered with Heroic Hearts Project for veteran-specific programs. Led by shamans and compassionate facilitators.
contact_info: Available on website; partners with Heroic Hearts
notes: 70% of veterans show significant PTSD improvement 90 days post-ceremony. Heroic Hearts partnership provides veteran-tailored approach.

name: Heroic Hearts Project - Mexico Retreats
description: Nonprofit founded 2017 by Army Ranger Jesse Gould helping 1,000+ veterans access psychedelics. Operates retreats in Mexico, Peru, Jamaica. Veteran-built Heroic Hearts Process includes screening, prep, ceremonies, guided integration, alumni support.
notes: 80%+ effectiveness in reducing PTSD symptoms. Veteran-founded and operated. Strong aftercare support.

name: The Hope Project
description: Empowers veteran families through coaching, support groups, resources, and research-proven psychedelic retreats. Partnered with Heroic Hearts. 5-7 day programs in countries where medicines legal. 80%+ show improvement vs 10% with standard therapy.
notes: Focused on veteran spouses, partners, Gold Star Wives. Joined forces with Heroic Hearts in 2023.

name: Mexisoul Ayahuasca Retreats
description: Safe ayahuasca retreat in Mexico serving veterans and general population. Multiple ceremony options with experienced shamans and facilitators supporting healing.
notes: Veteran-friendly programs. Multiple location options.

name: Bluestone Ayahuasca Retreats
description: Ayahuasca retreat center in Cancun with experienced shamans and facilitators. Multi-day programs with ceremonies supporting PTSD and trauma healing.
notes: Cancun location convenient for US travel.

name: MindScape Retreat
description: Full-range psychedelic treatment modalities in Cozumel administered under board-certified physician supervision. Integrates multiple psychedelic substances for comprehensive healing.
notes: Island location supports healing. Multiple substance options.

name: Arkana International - Valle de Bravo, Mexico
description: Psychedelic retreats in mystical Valle de Bravo region with compassionate facilitators. Multiple sacred plant medicine ceremonies with spiritual practices supporting healing.
notes: Multiple retreat locations. Mix of traditional and clinical approaches.

name: Buena Vida Retreats
description: Since 2018, guided 1,800+ guests through personalized psychedelic work with compassion and professional care. Deeply personal healing journeys with experienced facilitators.
notes: Long track record. Proven track record of successful outcomes.

name: Holistic Bio Spa
description: Ketamine therapy in Puerto Vallarta, Nuevo Vallarta. Cost 50-80% lower than USA. Rapid antidepressant effects, some showing benefits within hours.
notes: RESKUE program serves veterans/first responders/military. 50-80% cost savings.

name: Ketamine Health Centers
description: Multiple ketamine treatment facilities in Mexico offering IV and Spravato treatments for treatment-resistant mental health disorders. RESKUE program specifically serves veterans and first responders.
notes: RESKUE program for military/veterans/first responders.

name: Baja Ibogaine Center
description: Medically supervised ketamine infusion therapy for depression, PTSD, trauma. 1-2 week intensive programs in Baja California with medical oversight.
notes: Specializes in trauma and PTSD treatment.

name: Holistic Sanctuary
treatment_type: Holistic healing with plant medicine option
description: Luxury holistic treatment center with personalized programs. Pouyan Method combines sacred plant medicine, evidence-based science, holistic therapies. Up to 150 hrs/month individual care including yoga, massage, reiki, meditation, IV therapy.
notes: Luxury accommodations with intensive individual care.

name: Sanctuary Tulum
treatment_type: Holistic therapy with optional plant medicine
description: Luxury rehab blending evidence-based therapy with holistic/alternative healing. Counseling, mindfulness, yoga, nutritional therapy, optional plant medicine under medical supervision.
notes: Whole-person healing approach. Optional plant medicine integration.

name: Rythmia Life Advancement Center
description: Only medically licensed facility in the world authorized to serve ayahuasca as core therapeutic modality. Board of directors includes Kelly Slater, MLK III. 7-day data-driven program integrating plant medicine, spirituality, modern science. Four ayahuasca ceremonies, yoga, breathwork.
notes: Only medically licensed ayahuasca facility globally. Licensed by Costa Rican Ministry of Health.

name: Arkana International - Costa Rica
description: Arkana Costa Rica location set on acres overlooking Lake Arenal near Arenal Volcano. Ecological sanctuary for conscious living, deep healing, reconnection with nature. Ayahuasca ceremonies with experienced facilitators.
notes: Scenic location supporting healing. Connected to larger Arkana network.

name: Sacha Wasi - Ecuador
description: Ayahuasca retreats specifically designed for veterans with PTSD. Amazon rainforest location with qualified medical professionals and indigenous healers (some of whom are war veterans). Brotherhood of veterans supporting each other.
notes: Indigenous Yachak healers including war veterans. Veteran community support.

name: Feather Crown - Ecuador
description: Since 2008, offering responsibly guided sacred plant medicine retreats with reputable indigenous shamans in Ecuadorian Amazon. Healing center with experienced facilitators.
notes: Long-established (since 2008). Indigenous shamans.

name: Amayas Ecuador
description: Immersive transformative experiences in Ecuadorian jungle. 7-day gentle and restorative retreat or extended options. Three traditional ayahuasca ceremonies guided by skilled shamans.
notes: Multiple retreat length options. Traditional indigenous approach.

name: Gaia Sagrada - Ecuador
description: Ayahuasca retreat center near Cuenca offering low-cost, safe healing experiences. Beautiful natural setting. Transformative plant medicine work.
notes: Budget-friendly option. Easy visitor access.

name: Casa del Sol - Ecuador
description: Ancestral journey retreats combining plant medicine ceremonies with integration tools. 5 and 9-day options guided by indigenous wisdom keepers in beautiful natural setting.
notes: Combines ancestral wisdom with modern integration. Multiple retreat lengths.

name: Blue Morpho - Peru
description: Guided by Maestro Hamilton Souther. Led 15,000+ people through trauma-release and personal growth since 2002. 6-day all-inclusive experience with 4 ceremonies. Options: Amazon rainforest or Sacred Valley.
contact_info: Available on website; veteran facilitator available (Christian Gearhart, Navy Vet)
notes: Veteran co-founder (US Navy Vet). Established retreat center.

name: Temple of the Way of Light - Peru
description: Traditional plant-medicine shamanic healing center in Peruvian Amazon. Intensive ayahuasca retreats with female and male Shipibo healers. Minimum 12-day program for deep healing work.
notes: Longer program allows for deeper integration. Shipibo healers.

name: Arkana Spiritual Center - Peru
description: Part of larger Arkana network serving Heroic Hearts and other veterans. Plant medicine ceremonies with experienced shamans and facilitators.
notes: Connected to Heroic Hearts partnerships.

name: Ayahuasca-Retreat - Colombia
description: 5-star ayahuasca retreat 55 minutes from Medellin. Veteran-specific programs with support and integration pathways. Facilitators include veterans themselves offering shared experience guidance. Monthly community calls for continued accountability.
notes: Veteran facilitators. Ongoing community support for veterans.

name: Eagle Condor Alliance - Colombia
treatment_type: Ayahuasca (Yagé), San Pedro, Peyote, Temazcal ceremonies
description: 10-day sacred medicine retreats in mountains outside Medellin. Multiple plant medicine options with traditional ceremonies supporting deep healing work.
notes: Multiple plant medicine options in one retreat.

name: LaWayra - Colombia
description: Affordable ayahuasca retreats near Medellin. Experienced shamans and facilitators supporting healing for diverse populations.
notes: Budget-friendly near Medellin.

name: Gaya Kali Center - Brazil
treatment_type: Ayahuasca, Iboga, Bufo, Kambo with integration therapy
description: Trauma-recovery and plant medicine retreat center. 10-day immersive healing combining multiple psychoactive substances with somatic work, integration therapy, compassionate guidance.
notes: Multiple substance options. Trauma-specific focus.

name: Spirit Vine - Brazil
description: 9-day retreats in Bahia, Brazil. Run by former clinical psychologist. Non-indigenous but deeply spiritual ceremonies in safe environment. Combines ayahuasca with guided meditation, regression workshops, art integration.
notes: Affordable option. Clinical psychologist background.

name: TheraPsil - Canada
description: B.C.-based nonprofit advocating therapeutic psilocybin use. Supports Canadian veterans/patients accessing psilocybin through clinical trials, Section 56 Exemptions, Special Access Program. Training MDMA-assisted therapists.
notes: Non-profit advocacy organization. Navigates Canadian regulatory pathways. Costs not covered by VAC.

name: MAPS Canada
description: Canadian branch of international MAPS organization. Supporting beneficial uses of psychedelic medicines. Research on psilocybin, MDMA, ketamine. Focus on health care workers and first responders mental wellness.
notes: Research-focused organization. First responder focus.

name: Psyon - Czech Republic
description: First psychedelic-assisted therapy clinic in Europe, established 2019. Treated 1,000+ patients with ketamine therapy. Discussing MAPS MDMA trial participation. Addressing Ukrainian war veteran PTSD needs.
notes: First European clinic. Ukrainian war veteran focus. MAPS trial involvement.

name: Liminal Minds Clinic - Portugal
description: First psychedelic-assisted therapy clinic in Portugal, opened January 2022. Partnership with Saint John of God Clinic in central Lisbon. KAP indicated for treatment-resistant depression.
notes: Pioneering clinic in Portugal. Hospital partnership.

name: Alchemy of Clarity - Portugal
description: Psilocybin retreats in Portugal. Legal gray area but operations continue. Preparation and integration support provided.
notes: Portugal decriminalized but not legalized. Legal gray area.

name: Vine of the Soul Retreats - Portugal
description: Trauma-informed ayahuasca retreats in Europe. Specialized for trauma healing with experienced facilitators and integration support.
notes: Trauma specialization. Multiple European locations.

name: OVID Clinics Berlin - Germany
description: One of first two institutions in Germany/EU authorized to treat depression with psilocybin. Ketamine-assisted therapy. Psilocybin clinical trials ongoing. Compassionate use program for severe cases.
notes: Pioneering German clinic. Clinical trial participation.

name: Charité Hospital Berlin - Germany
description: Berlin's Charité hospital running psilocybin clinical trials for treatment-resistant depression. Scientific medical institution conducting rigorous research.
notes: Academic medical center. Rigorous clinical research.

name: Central Institute for Mental Health - Germany
description: Mannheim-based institute. First German Compassionate Use Program for psilocybin. EPIsoDE study: 144 patients in randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial combining psilocybin with psychotherapy.
notes: National-level program. Major clinical trial (EPIsoDE).

name: King's College London - Psychoactive Trials Group
description: Psychoactive Trials Group at prestigious medical university. Controlled clinical trials with psychedelics. 60% of PTSD sufferers no longer met criteria for PTSD after treatment in similar trials.
notes: Elite university research. Veteran trial involvement.

name: UK Armed Forces Psilocybin Study
description: Feasibility study exploring safety of psilocybin-assisted psychotherapy (PAP) for UK military veterans with PTSD. Two dosing sessions of 25mg psilocybin followed by Cognitive Processing Therapy.
website: Available through King's College London or PTSD UK
notes: UK-specific veteran program. Rigorous feasibility study.

name: Sykehuset Østfold - Norway
description: Norwegian hospital study site for MAPS Phase II MDMA-assisted therapy for PTSD. First proof-of-principle study on MDMA for major depressive disorder completed in Norway.
notes: MAPS trial site. Early MDMA research.

name: University of Oslo - Norway
description: Norwegian university conducting clinical trials with psilocybin and MDMA for mental health conditions. Part of Nordic psychedelic research network.
notes: Academic medical research center.

name: Swiss Special Access Program (PAT) - Switzerland
description: Swiss Federal Act allows restricted medical use of scheduled drugs for treatment-resistant cases. Since 2014, 50 licenses granted. Psychedelic-assisted group therapy using MDMA then LSD for complex PTSD.
contact_info: Available through MAPS and Swiss medical centers
notes: Only PAT globally allowing LSD medically. Unique therapeutic protocol.

name: US Department of Veterans Affairs - Psychedelic Research
description: VA approved funding for psychedelic-assisted psychotherapy clinical trials. Nine VA facilities participating. First VA-funded psychedelic research since 1960s. MDMA and psilocybin trials ongoing.

location.city: Bronx, LA, Omaha, Palo Alto, Portland, San Diego, SF, West Haven, White River Junction

cost_range: Clinical trial enrollment (free for VA members)
contact_info: Available through local VA medical centers
notes: Federal government funding. Major landmark decision. 67% no longer met PTSD criteria after MDMA therapy.

name: VA Foreign Medical Program (FMP)
treatment_type: Medical care abroad for service-connected conditions
description: VA assumes payment for treatment of service-connected disabilities in Mexico and worldwide. Covers medications, services, equipment. No copays/deductibles for eligible veterans.
notes: Free enrollment. Only covers service-connected conditions. Alternative to commercial clinics.

name: Lakeside Medical Group - Mexico
description: American veteran-owned clinic accepting FMP, TRICARE, CHAMPVA, Medicare Advantage. Operates in Mexico since 2013. Dedicated to US veterans and families in Mexico.
cost_range: Variable by service; FMP covers service-connected
notes: Accepts FMP. Veteran-owned. Multiple Mexico locations.

name: US Veterans Medical Care in Mexico
description: Organization providing diagnostic studies and medical care to US veterans and families in Mexico. Dedicated to high-quality private medical services.
notes: Mexico City based. Private medical services.
